title: Audit memory verify-payload runtime reducibility

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Audit whether memory.verify-payload.report can be reduced from package-domain runtime to portable primitives in a bounded #892 slice.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not port payload verification by copying installer.verify_payload policy into command-generation or generated runtime. If the behavior requires a new declarative verification IR, record no-safe-route and continue the epic.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Inspect the operation contract, installer.verify_payload, focused tests, and inventory; implement only if the replacement is a small composition of existing or clearly generic primitives.
canonical_core.escalate_when: Payload verification requires domain-specific policy tables, upgrade-source validation, guidance-fragment assertions, or broad result assembly not already represented declaratively.
canonical_core.next_action: Complete reducibility audit and either implement a bounded primitive reduction or close as no-safe-route with continuation evidence.
proof_expectations: Focused inspection identifies whether verify-payload is generic primitive work or domain verification policy.
If no-safe-route, close with evidence and keep #892 decomposition updated.
completion_criteria: verify-payload is either reduced through portable primitives with proof, or explicitly retained as domain-runtime debt with a concrete migration shape.
The broader #892 lane remains open unless full intent is proven.

goal: Create a bounded plan for Audit memory verify-payload runtime reducibility.
non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

#1007 captures the required declarative verification IR follow-up.

execution_run.what happened: Audited installer.verify_payload, the operation contract, and tests. The behavior is broad deterministic payload verification policy rather than a bounded primitive replacement.
execution_run.scope touched: Planning artefacts and GitHub issue follow-up only.
execution_run.changed surfaces: No executable code changed. Filed #1007 for declarative payload-verification IR before portable codegen.
execution_run.result for continuation: continue #892; do not reduce memory.verify-payload by copying installer policy into command-generation.
execution_run.next step: Archive this audit and promote the next bounded #892 implementation slice.

finished_run_review.intent served: yes: no-safe-route was identified and routed

delegation_outcome_feedback.expected savings: Open worker reviews verify-payload reducibility and stop conditions while local agent inspects implementation shape.
delegation_outcome_feedback.quality concern: No edits delegated; implementation proceeds only if portable primitive scope is genuinely bounded.

proof_report.validation proof: Focused inspection of installer.verify_payload and tests; planning summary after archive.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: verify_payload checks version metadata, upgrade-source records, required/forbidden payload files, current-memory baseline, contract surface summaries, and guidance fragments; #1007 tracks modeling that policy declaratively.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: #892 Python generated CLI completion: keep reducing package runtime fallbacks only when behavior can be represented by declarative IR and generic primitives.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: The audit prevents unsafe scope shrinking for one candidate; full #892 remains open.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: memory.verify-payload retained as explicit domain-runtime debt pending declarative verification IR
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: The audit result is preserved in the #892 decomposition and #1007.
execution_summary.resume from: select the next #892 reducible runtime fallback from the decomposition

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. Prefer `agentic-planning archive-plan <plan> --prepare-closeout` before hand-editing closeout fields.
closure_check.why this decision is honest: A quick primitive reduction would copy verification policy into codegen; #1007 is needed before this can be portable.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: installer.verify_payload owns multiple verification-policy families that should be modeled declaratively.
closure_check.reopen trigger: #1007 is ready for implementation or verify-payload behavior is narrowed to a bounded primitive composition.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, separate acted-on, reported-only/routed, and dismissed incidental findings; keep this compact and evidence-backed.
